company_profile.name: Good Times Restaurants Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Good Times Restaurants Inc., through its subsidiaries, engages in the restaurant business in the United States. It owns, operates, and franchises Good Times Burgers & Frozen Custard, a quick-service drive-through dining restaurant; and owns, operates, franchises, and licenses Bad Daddy's Burger Bar, a full-service upscale casual dining restaurant. Good Times Restaurants Inc. was incorporated in 1987 and is based in Golden, Colorado.
company_profile.description_summary:
This company operates two restaurant brands in the United States. Good Times Burgers & Frozen Custard is a quick-service drive-through concept, while Bad Daddy's Burger Bar offers upscale casual dining. Revenue comes from owned and operated locations as well as franchise and licensing arrangements.

The company's distinctive positioning combines a fast-casual drive-through burger concept with a premium full-service dining brand, allowing it to serve different market segments within the burger restaurant category.

company_profile.name: Gulf Resources, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Cyclical business (basic materials) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
company_profile.description: Gulf Resources, Inc., through its subsidiaries, manufactures and trades in bromine and crude salt in the People's Republic of China. The company operates through four segments: Bromine, Crude Salt, Chemical Products, and Natural Gas. It provides bromine for the manufacturing of various bromine compounds for applications in industry and agriculture, intermediates in organic synthesis, brominated flame retardants, fumigants, water purification compounds, dyes, medicines, and disinfectants. The company also offers crude salt for applications in alkali and chlorine alkali production, as well as chemical, food and beverage, and other industries. In addition, it manufactures and sells chemical products for use in oil and gas field exploration, oil and gas distribution, oil field drilling, papermaking chemical agents, and inorganic chemicals; and materials that are used for human and animal antibiotics. Gulf Resources, Inc. is headquartered in Shouguang, the People's Republic of China.
company_profile.description_summary:
This company manufactures and trades bromine and crude salt in China through four operating segments. It produces bromine for applications in flame retardants, fumigants, water purification, dyes, and medicines, while crude salt serves alkali production and food industries. The company also manufactures chemical products for oil and gas exploration and pharmaceutical materials.

The company's distinctive strength lies in its integrated portfolio spanning multiple chemical segments, positioning it across bromine production, salt extraction, specialized chemicals for energy sectors, and pharmaceutical intermediates.

_meta.type_rationale: Cyclical business (basic materials) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
_meta.method_explanation: Cyclical companies — such as semiconductors, mining, or manufacturing — have profits that rise sharply in good times and fall sharply in downturns. This makes earnings-based methods like P/E unreliable, because the earnings figure at any single point in time can be unusually high or low. EV/EBITDA smooths out some of this distortion by looking at earnings before interest and tax, making it a fairer comparison across the economic cycle. Price-to-Sales is also useful as revenue tends to be more stable than profits for cyclical businesses.

_meta.type_rationale: Bank or thrift — P/Tangible Book prioritised; DCF excluded
_meta.method_explanation: Banks make money from the difference between what they charge on loans and what they pay on deposits — not from traditional business cash flows. Because of this, standard cash flow models (DCF) do not work well for banks. Instead, we focus on Price-to-Book (P/B), which compares the stock price to the value of the bank's assets on its books. Banks also carry a lot of debt by design — they borrow money to lend it out — so high leverage is normal and not a red flag.

The company offers wireless communication technologies, such as 5G/5.75G/4.5G/4G transceivers and modems; RF and modem chipsets based on fourth-generation (4G) long term evolution (LTE) technology, including 4G LTE, 4.5G LTE Advanced, and 4.75G LTE Advanced-Pro; baseband modem solutions, RF transceivers, and system-on-chip (SoC) solutions; and product design and support, warranty development, customer support, and technical support services. It also develops and sells cellular Internet of Things (IoT) chipsets for low-speed mobile networks, such as eMTC/NB-IOT/Sigfox, and other network protocols; and 5G NR chipsets and other 5G NR solutions. The company's products and solutions are used in portable wireless routers, indoor and outdoor fixed wireless routers, industrial machine-to-machine applications, smartphones, fixed wireless subscriber terminals, various communication modules and devices, and industrial products. It sells its products directly or indirectly through distributors to wireless operators, original equipment manufacturers (OEMs), and original design manufacturers (ODMs) in Taiwan, China, Korea, Japan, Europe, North America, and South America. GCT Semiconductor Holding, Inc. has strategic partnership with MaxLinear, Inc. to develop 5G fixed wireless access (FWA) gateways and converged gateways for consumer and enterprise applications. GCT Semiconductor Holding, Inc. was founded in 1998 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.
company_profile.description_summary:
A fabless semiconductor company, GCT designs and sells communication semiconductors for industrial, business-to-business, and consumer applications globally. It develops 5G/4G transceivers, modems, baseband solutions, RF chipsets, and cellular IoT chipsets used in routers, smartphones, and machine-to-machine devices. Revenue comes from direct and indirect sales to wireless operators, OEMs, and ODMs.

The company partners with MaxLinear to develop 5G fixed wireless access gateways, distinguishing its strategy through collaborative innovation in emerging connectivity solutions.

_meta.type_rationale: Cyclical business (technology) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
_meta.method_explanation: Cyclical companies — such as semiconductors, mining, or manufacturing — have profits that rise sharply in good times and fall sharply in downturns. This makes earnings-based methods like P/E unreliable, because the earnings figure at any single point in time can be unusually high or low. EV/EBITDA smooths out some of this distortion by looking at earnings before interest and tax, making it a fairer comparison across the economic cycle. Price-to-Sales is also useful as revenue tends to be more stable than profits for cyclical businesses.

_notes.returns_trajectory: Return on Equity (ROE) measures how much profit a company generates for every dollar of shareholder money invested. A company consistently earning above 15% ROIC is generally considered to have a genuine competitive advantage over its peers.
_notes.revenue_vs_ar: Accounts receivable is money the company is owed by customers who have not yet paid their bills. This chart compares revenue growth to accounts receivable growth each year. A healthy company should see both lines move at a similar pace.

company_profile.name: Genesis Energy, L.P.
company_profile.type_rationale: Cyclical business (energy) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
company_profile.description: Genesis Energy, L.P. engages in the midstream segment of the crude oil and natural gas industry in the United States. It operates through Offshore Pipeline Transportation; Marine Transportation; and Onshore Facilities and Transportation segments. The Offshore Pipeline Transportation segment engages in offshore crude oil and natural gas pipeline transportation and handling operations, as well as provision of a suite of services to integrated and large independent energy companies. This segment also owns interests in offshore crude oil and natural gas pipeline systems, platforms, and related infrastructure. The Marine Transportation segment includes inland marine fleet, which transports intermediate refined petroleum products, such as asphalt; offshore marine fleet, which transports crude oil and refined petroleum products; and M/T American Phoenix, a modern, double-hulled tanker. The Onshore Facilities and Transportation segment provides transportation and facilities services to crude oil refiners and producers by purchasing, transporting, storing, blending, and marketing crude oil and refined products; and owns a portfolio of logistical assets consisting of pipelines, trucks, tanks and terminals, barges and rail unloading facilities. This segment also owns and operates onshore common carrier crude oil pipeline systems and operational crude oil rail unloading facilities; and is involved in processing of high sulfur gas streams for refineries, as well as selling of related by-product, sodium hydrosulfide. Genesis Energy, L.P. was incorporated in 1996 and is headquartered in Houston, Texas.
company_profile.description_summary:
Genesis Energy operates in the midstream energy sector, transporting and handling crude oil and natural gas across the United States. Its revenue comes from offshore pipeline transportation, marine transportation of petroleum products via inland and offshore fleets, and onshore facilities providing storage, blending, and logistics services for crude oil and refined products.

The company distinguishes itself through diversified infrastructure assets spanning pipelines, terminals, trucks, barges, and rail facilities. It also processes high-sulfur gas streams for refineries and produces sodium hydrosulfide as a by-product.

company_profile.name: Green Dot Corporation
company_profile.type_rationale: Consumer finance / payments — DNI and P/B prioritised; standard debt ratios unsuitable due to balance sheet structure
company_profile.description: Green Dot Corporation, a financial technology and registered bank holding company, provides various financial services to consumers and businesses in the United States. The company operates through three segments: Consumer Services, Business to Business Services, and Money Movement Services. It provides deposit account programs, including consumer and small business checking account products, network-branded reloadable prepaid debit cards and gift cards, and secured credit programs. The company offers money processing services, such as cash transfer services that enable consumers to deposit or pick up cash and pay bills with cash at the point-of-sale at any participating retailer; and disbursement services, which enable wages and authorized funds disbursement to its deposit account programs and accounts issued by any third-party bank or program manager. In addition, it offers tax processing services consisting of tax refund transfers, which provide the processing technology to facilitate receipt of a taxpayers' refund proceeds; small business lending to independent tax preparation providers that seek small advances; and fast cash advance, a loan that enables tax refund recipients. Green Dot Corporation was incorporated in 1999 and is headquartered in Provo, Utah.
company_profile.description_summary:
Green Dot Corporation is a financial technology and bank holding company offering deposit accounts, reloadable prepaid debit cards, and gift cards to consumers and businesses. Revenue streams include money processing services like cash transfers and bill payments at retail locations, wage disbursement services, and tax processing solutions including refund transfers and small business lending to tax preparers.

The company operates distinctively across three segments—Consumer Services, Business to Business Services, and Money Movement Services—enabling both individual consumers and businesses to access financial products and services through multiple channels and partner networks.

_meta.type_rationale: Consumer finance / payments — DNI and P/B prioritised; standard debt ratios unsuitable due to balance sheet structure
_meta.method_explanation: Consumer finance companies — such as credit card providers or personal lenders — earn income from the interest they charge on loans. Like banks, they carry a lot of debt by design because lending money is their core business model. We use net income DCF and Price-to-Book, which together capture both the earning power and the asset value of the loan book.

company_profile.name: The GEO Group, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: The GEO Group, Inc. is a leading diversified government service provider, specializing in design, financing, development, and support services for secure facilities, processing centers, and community reentry centers in the United States, Australia, South Africa, and the United Kingdom. GEO's diversified services include enhanced in-custody rehabilitation and post-release support through the award-winning GEO Continuum of Care, secure transportation, electronic monitoring, community-based programs, and correctional health and mental health care. GEO's worldwide operations include the ownership and/or delivery of support services for 95 facilities totaling approximately 75,000 beds, including idle facilities and projects under development, with a workforce of up to approximately 20,000 employees. The GEO Group, Inc. was incorporated in 1984 and is based in Boca Raton, United States.
company_profile.description_summary:
This company operates as a diversified government services provider specializing in secure facilities, processing centers, and community reentry centers across the United States, Australia, South Africa, and the United Kingdom. It generates revenue through designing, financing, and developing correctional facilities, along with providing support services including rehabilitation, electronic monitoring, secure transportation, and health care.

The company distinguishes itself through its integrated GEO Continuum of Care model, offering comprehensive in-custody rehabilitation and post-release support services. It operates approximately 95 facilities worldwide with roughly 75,000 beds.

_meta.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
_meta.method_explanation: This company does not fit neatly into a specialist category, so we apply a balanced mix of all ten valuation methods. These include Discounted Cash Flow (which estimates the present value of future cash flows), earnings multiples (P/E and PEG), revenue multiples (P/S and PSG), book value (P/B), and EV/EBITDA. Each method approaches value from a different angle — using all of them together gives a more rounded estimate than relying on any single number.

bull_points: GEO reported record new business wins of up to $520 million in annualized revenues in 2025, and management expects 2026 to remain active with upside potential across segments, particularly from 6,000 available high-security idle beds and expanded electronic monitoring services.

Q4 2025 revenue exceeded expectations by 2.95% with strong 23% year-over-year growth driven by new contracts and expansions; management raised full-year 2026 guidance to $525-545M Adjusted EBITDA, signaling confidence in contract ramp.

Stock is trading at $23.39 versus an intrinsic value of $17.47, but analyst price targets of $37 represent 58% upside; bullish analysts frame lower targets as a reset, not a reversal, citing multi-year contract wins supporting a path to $3B+ annualized revenue.
bear_points: Q4 2025 EPS of $0.25 missed forecast of $0.27 by 7.41%, triggering a 13.77% stock drop; management's modest Q1 2026 guidance of $107-112M Adjusted EBITDA reflects front-loaded payroll taxes and transition headwinds from the Skip Tracing pilot program.

Analyst price targets were slashed from $41.80 to $37, with Simply Wall St noting analysts trimmed forecasts due to revised growth estimates and softer profit outlook, indicating Street skepticism about near-term margin expansion.

The company's debt load of $1.65 billion at year-end 2025 against just $70M cash creates refinancing risk; debt-to-EBITDA ratio of 3.62x remains elevated, constraining financial flexibility to pursue aggressive capital returns or M&A.

_meta.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 23.7%; DCF methods prioritised
_meta.method_explanation: Free cash flow compounders are businesses that consistently generate more cash than they need to run the business — think of companies like consumer goods brands or software firms with subscription revenue. Because their cash flows are reliable year after year, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model works very well. DCF asks: if I add up all the future cash flows this company will generate and adjust for the time value of money, what is it worth today? For these companies, that calculation gives a meaningful and trustworthy answer.

metric_assessments.profitability: GE Aerospace is delivering exceptional profitability, with a 17.7% profit margin and a 45.43% return on equity—both far above typical industrial companies, which usually see margins in the 8–12% range and ROE in the 15–20% range. The limiting factor is that growth is now constrained—the company has noted internal or external headwinds that will slow expansion from here, preventing it from sustaining the explosive growth rates seen recently.
metric_assessments.predictability: Earnings and cash flow visibility are weak, with only a 3/10 score, meaning quarter-to-quarter or year-to-year results are likely volatile and harder to forecast. Aerospace supply chains are inherently subject to lumpy order patterns, customer production schedules, and external shocks, making it difficult for investors to predict what earnings will look like in 12–24 months. This unpredictability makes the stock riskier to own and harder to value, and it also limits the company's ability to command a premium valuation—investors price in a risk discount when they cannot see earnings clearly.
metric_assessments.valuation: At a 3/10 score, the stock appears expensive relative to its fundamentals and growth prospects, likely trading at a higher price-to-earnings or price-to-book multiple than comparable industrial peers. Despite strong profitability and growth, weak predictability and a constrained growth outlook leave little margin of safety for new buyers at current prices. company_profile.description: Griffon Corporation, through its subsidiaries, provides home and building, and consumer and professional products in the United States, Europe, Canada, Australia, and internationally. This segment also sells garage door openers. _meta.type_rationale: Cyclical business (basic materials) — EV/EBITDA and P/S prioritised over P/E
_meta.method_explanation: Cyclical companies — such as semiconductors, mining, or manufacturing — have profits that rise sharply in good times and fall sharply in downturns. This makes earnings-based methods like P/E unreliable, because the earnings figure at any single point in time can be unusually high or low. EV/EBITDA smooths out some of this distortion by looking at earnings before interest and tax, making it a fairer comparison across the economic cycle. Price-to-Sales is also useful as revenue tends to be more stable than profits for cyclical businesses.
